currentState: Chapter 8 turns them from senior-junior alignment into local-and-field continuity partners. Jaina still outranks him absolutely, but she now trusts him with aftermath work instead of treating him only as access to the current answer.
powerDynamic: Jaina still holds the larger magical and strategic synthesis. Corin now holds the survival of the evidence inside the city they are leaving behind. Her authority directs him; his local stewardship keeps her breakthrough from dying the moment she rides out.

physicalDynamics.pattern: He nearly trips over rank and title until she cuts the panic short; after that they work shoulder-to-shoulder over route sheets and residue trays with paper acting as the stabilizer between them.
Their strongest chapter 8 rhythm is transitional: Jaina moves from using Corin's fragments to leaning on him as the man who will keep the records split once she is gone.

nameUsage.jainaCallsCorin: Still uses his name plainly, now with the added weight of assignment rather than only reassurance.
nameUsage.corinCallsJaina: Less rank-spilled than before; by chapter end he speaks to her as someone receiving hard work, not begging to be useful.

history.event: Corin reveals he has been tracking route irregularities and ward flares for weeks, and Jaina immediately treats that buried work as credible.
He opens the residue-room lane for her and supports the repack-floor solve that proves the hub-treatment method.
Corin's local rumor and academic memory help Jaina turn the dead operative's broken clue into Kel'Thuzad's name.
Jaina folds Corin into the aftermath plan, ordering the records split and kept in multiple caches rather than treating him as one more evacuee.

trajectory: Ignored local functionary -> validated field contact -> trusted local steward of the evidence.

stateHistory.currentState: Immediate senior-junior alignment. Corin begins in rank panic and Jaina strips it out of him fast, turning him into a working partner rather than a trembling subordinate.
